text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. Clean code is readable code that others can understand easily. Good naming conventions are very important; use clear names for variables and functions so that their purpose is obvious. For example, instead of naming a variable "a", name it "userAge". Functions should do one thing and do it well. If a function is too long, break it into smaller, focused functions. This makes your code easier to test and update later. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, saving time and effort for yourself and your team.
